signals.reasoning: BTC is stable enough, but the board is still under an active veto and fresh entries do not have a clean enough edge to justify acting through the event brake.
ETH has recovered with the broader tape, but the live event veto still matters more than the local improvement. Neutral is the disciplined call here.
SOL is participating in the rebound, but the move still looks headline-sensitive rather than like a clean durable breakout. Best to stay flat under veto.
STABLE still has one of the strongest raw squeeze profiles on the board, but that does not override a live veto regime. Best raw long is not the same as best trade now.
CHILLGUY looks constructive, but it is extended enough that the active event brake matters more than the local momentum. Watch it, do not force it.
WCT has some squeeze and trend support characteristics, but the execution quality is still messy and the veto makes it too easy to get trapped chasing strength.
BOME is too thin and too noisy to trust through a headline-driven regime. There is no reason to force a trade here.
ZEC still has bearish structure, but the move is already extended enough that a fresh short is less attractive under a live veto. Better to stay neutral than chase.
TST has bounced sharply, but it still reads more like noisy recovery than a clean actual long. The veto keeps it in watch-only territory.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
JTO still has a real squeeze setup on paper, but the live veto keeps it from being a clean actual long. It belongs on the watchlist rather than in the buy column.
